# hush_rabbitmq_access_privilege (Resource)

Manage RabbitMQ access privileges in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a RabbitMQ access privilege
resource "hush_rabbitmq_access_privilege" "example" {
  name        = "my-rabbitmq-privilege"
  description = "RabbitMQ privilege with full access to default vhost"

  permissions {
    vhost     = "/"
    configure = ""         # no permission to create/delete resources
    write     = "app\\..*" # publish only to app.* exchanges/queues
    read      = ".*"       # consume from any queue
  }

  tags = ["monitoring"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the RabbitMQ access privilege
- `permissions` (Block List, Min: 1) The RabbitMQ permission entries (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--permissions))

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the RabbitMQ access privilege
- `tags` (List of String) The list of RabbitMQ user tags

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the RabbitMQ access privilege
- `type` (String) The type of access privilege

<a id="nestedblock--permissions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `permissions`

Required:

- `vhost` (String) The RabbitMQ virtual host

Optional:

- `configure` (String) The configure permission pattern (regex)
- `read` (String) The read permission pattern (regex)
- `write` (String) The write permission pattern (regex)
